rule.description: CIS Ubuntu Linux 24.04 LTS Benchmark v1.0.0.: Ensure all AppArmor Profiles are in enforce or complain mode.: Status changed from passed to failed

sca.policy: CIS Ubuntu Linux 24.04 LTS Benchmark v1.0.0.

check.title: Ensure all AppArmor Profiles are in enforce or complain mode.
check.description: AppArmor profiles define what resources applications are able to access.
check.rationale: Security configuration requirements vary from site to site. Some sites may mandate a policy that is stricter than the default policy, which is perfectly acceptable. This item is intended to ensure that any policies that exist on the system are activated.
check.remediation: Run the following command to set all profiles to enforce mode: # aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/* - OR - Run the following command to set all profiles to complain mode: # aa-complain /etc/apparmor.d/* Note: Any unconfined processes may need to have a profile created or activated for them and then be restarted.

rule.description: CIS Ubuntu Linux 24.04 LTS Benchmark v1.0.0.: Ensure all AppArmor Profiles are in enforce or complain mode.: Status changed from failed to passed

sca.policy: CIS Ubuntu Linux 24.04 LTS Benchmark v1.0.0.

check.title: Ensure all AppArmor Profiles are in enforce or complain mode.
check.description: AppArmor profiles define what resources applications are able to access.
check.rationale: Security configuration requirements vary from site to site. Some sites may mandate a policy that is stricter than the default policy, which is perfectly acceptable. This item is intended to ensure that any policies that exist on the system are activated.
check.remediation: Run the following command to set all profiles to enforce mode: # aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/* - OR - Run the following command to set all profiles to complain mode: # aa-complain /etc/apparmor.d/* Note: Any unconfined processes may need to have a profile created or activated for them and then be restarted.
